These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the {{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702年]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702年]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qqq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|後白河天皇}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭)== See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2年） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:Tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}
