The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:11]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:kk]] {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]][[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. {{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社|12=draft}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|12=draft}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|12=draft}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|12=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|12=draft}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|12=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|12=draft}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|12=draft}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=draft}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=draft}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=draft}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=draft}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=draft}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=draft}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|12=draft}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=draft}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=draft}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=draft}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|12=draft}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|12=draft}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|12=draft}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=draft}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=draft}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|12=draft}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|12=draft}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:To be resorted]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社|12=draft}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|12=draft}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|12=draft}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|12=draft}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|12=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|12=draft}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|12=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|12=draft}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|12=draft}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=draft}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=draft}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=draft}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=draft}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=draft}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=draft}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|12=draft}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=draft}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=draft}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=draft}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=draft}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|12=draft}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|12=draft}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|12=draft}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=draft}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=draft}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|12=draft}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=draft}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|12=draft}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{citation needed|Otaki Ontake Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}}

47 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:王滝御嶽神社 up to rev 101697330
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 王滝御嶽神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
里宮の本殿拝殿 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]3315番地（王滝御嶽神社別殿） |位置 = 里宮：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
奥社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = 里宮、奥社：[[国常立尊]] [[大己貴命]] [[少彦名命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[702年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年） |本殿 = 御嶽神社・里宮 |別名 = 御嶽神社王滝口、王滝口御嶽神社 |札所等 = [[#神社社殿・社務所|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所]]を参照 |例祭 = [[#祭事|王滝御嶽神社の祭事]]を参照 |神事 = 王滝御嶽神社の祭事を参照 |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Nagano|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=53.3|lon_deg=137|lon_min=33|lon_sec=7.2|position=bottom|label=里宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=53|lat_sec=19.4|lon_deg=137|lon_min=28|lon_sec=57.8|position=right|label=奥社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.847240|N|137.530747|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E}}|label1=里宮|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E}}|label2=奥社|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''王滝御嶽神社'''（おうたきおんたけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]にある神社。'''王滝口御嶽神社'''（おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ）、'''御嶽神社王滝口'''（おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち）ともいう。 [[木曽御嶽山]]への[[山岳信仰]]に基づく現地の神社（[[木曽御嶽神社]]）の1つであり、南方からの登山道である王滝口（おうたきぐち）沿いにある神社である（東方の黒沢口沿いにも[[黒沢御嶽神社|別の神社]]がある）。山頂に'''奥社'''、 山麓に'''里宮'''がある。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は以下の3柱。 [[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと） [[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと） [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと） 主祭神以外については、社殿の各宮の項目を参照。 == 歴史 == [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）、[[信濃]][[国司]]の'''高根道基'''が[[御嶽山 (長野県)|木曽御嶽山]]頂上奥社を創建したのを始まりとする。 [[宝亀]]5年（[[774年]]）、信濃国司の'''石川朝臣望足'''が、[[光仁天皇]]の勅命を奉じて[[御嶽山 (長野県)|木曽御嶽山]]へ登山し、悪疫退散を祈願した。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]3年（[[925年]]）、'''白川少将重頼'''が木曽御嶽山へ登山し奥社の神殿を再建した。 [[応保]]元年（[[1161年]]）、[[後白河天皇|後白河上皇]]の[[勅使]]が木曽御嶽山を登山し参拝した。 [[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、一合目里宮社を再建。 [[文亀]]3年（[[1503年]]）、一合目里宮社を再興したと記録にある。 [[寛文]]4年（[[1664年]]）、[[山村代官屋敷|木曽代官]]の[[山村良豊]]が里宮社殿を再建。 [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]）、[[覚明 (行者)|覚明]]が木曽御嶽山黒沢口登山道を開いた。 [[寛政]]4年（[[1792年]]）、[[普寛行者]]が木曽御嶽山王滝口登山道をそれぞれ開き、[[講社]]を作り軽精進潔斎による木曽御嶽山登山を奨励したことにより、御嶽講（[[山岳信仰]]・[[講]]）が全国に広まり、現在も[[御嶽教]]の信者による参拝が続いている。 == 社殿 == [[File:Ontakejinja satomiya0001.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社里宮]] [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 里宮社殿.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の里宮にある社殿]] [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 頂上奥社.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の王滝頂上奥社社殿]] *'''里宮'''（さとみや）一合目にあり、「本社（ほんしゃ）」「若宮（わかみや）」「岩戸権現（いわとごんげん）」とも呼ばれる。祭神は[[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと）・[[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと）・[[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと）。 *'''十二大権現'''（じゅうにだいごんげん）四合目下にあり、祭神は[[木花開耶姫命]]（このはなさくやびめのみこと）。 *'''八海山神社'''（はっかいさんじんじゃ）五合目にあり、祭神は[[国狭槌尊]]（くにさつちのみこと）。 *'''三笠山神社'''（みかさやまじんじゃ）七合目、三笠山頂上にあり、祭神は[[豊斟渟尊]]（とよくむぬのみこと）。 *'''田ノ原大黒天'''（たのはらだいこくてん）七合目にあり祭神は[[大国主命]]（おおくにぬしのみこと）。 *'''頂上奥社'''（ちょうじょうおくしゃ） 木曽御嶽山王滝口頂上にあり、祭神は[[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと）・[[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと）・[[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと）。 ==社務所== *別殿社務所・一合目 *里宮社務所・一合目 *里宮拝殿・一合目 *八海山社務所・五合目 *田の原社務所・七合目 *遙拝所社務所・七合目 *頂上社務所・[[木曽御嶽山]]王滝口頂上 == 祭事 == [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 例大祭.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の7月に行われる例大祭]] *年中行事 **1月1日 歳旦祭 里宮 **1月5日新年御神楽始め祭 別殿 **1月16日 厄除交通安全祈願祭 別殿 **2月1日 古神札焚上げ祭 里宮 **春分の日 春祭 里宮 **5月1日 合併神社祭 里宮
招魂社祭 招魂社 **6月30日 大祓式 別殿 **7月1日 開山祭 田の原 **7月10日 頂上奥社開山祭 頂上奥社 **7月27～28日 御嶽神社例大祭 里宮・八幡堂 **9月初旬 頂上奥社閉山祭 頂上奥社 **9月10日 講祖（[[普寛行者]]・[[覚明行者]]）本社祭 **10月第3土曜、日曜日 霊神社合祀慰霊大祭 里宮 **11月23日 秋祭 里宮 **12月31日 除夜祭 里宮 *毎日の祭事 **毎月1日および15日 月並祭 里宮・別殿 == 御嶽山信仰、行者、講社 == [[御嶽山 (長野県)#歴史・信仰|御嶽山の歴史・信仰]]および[[御嶽教]]を参照。 ===関連する神社・宗教法人=== *[[木曽御嶽本教]] *[[黒沢御嶽神社]] == 文化財等 == *古記録・絵画等 **[[祭文|古祭文]][[祝詞]]類 **王御嶽山清女行法巻 [[文亀]]3年（1503年） [[室町時代]]の古祭文 **角倉与一奉納絵馬 [[寛文]]8年（1668年） **千羽鶴絵馬 [[慶応]]2年（1866年） **龍虎の図 寛文4年（1664年） **天狗の面 **山村代官絵馬 **風流踊りの図 [[享保]]18年（1733年） *[[普寛行者]]ゆかりの品 **愛用の茶器と椀 **開山登山の節の護身用小刀 **普寛行者道中日記 **普寛行者一行に宛てた書状 **開山登山の節の護身用天狗の爪 == 現地情報 == [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]3315番地 別殿（滝旅館内） *交通アクセス **車利用の場合 [[東京]]方面からは、[[伊那インターチェンジ|伊那IC]]下車、里宮まで約55分 [[大阪]]・[[名古屋]]方面からは、[[中津川インターチェンジ|中津川IC]]下車、里宮まで1時間50分 **列車利用の場合 東京方面・大阪・名古屋方面いずれからも、[[中央本線]][[木曽福島]]下車、バス利用で里宮まで約50分 == 関連項目 == *[[御嶽山 (長野県)]] *[[王滝村]] *[[御嶽教]] *[[百草丸]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} *[http://www.ontakejinja.jp/ 王滝御嶽神社]（公式サイト） == 参考文献 == * 王滝村史 *『寺と神社 (信州の文化シリーズ)』　御嶽神社　p249　信州郷土史研究会　 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]]　1981年 * {{Cite book|和書 |editor=菅原壽清、中山郁、時枝務 |year=2009 |month=8 |title=木曽のおんたけさん―その歴史と信仰 |publisher=岩田書院 |isbn=978-4872945690 |ref=木曽のおんたけさん―その歴史と信仰}} == 脚注 == {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうたきおんたけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:王滝村の建築物]] [[Category:王滝村の歴史]] [[Category:山岳信仰]] [[Category:御嶽信仰]] [[Category:修験道]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[王滝御嶽神社]] to [[Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 王滝御嶽神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
里宮の本殿拝殿 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]3315番地（王滝御嶽神社別殿） |位置 = 里宮：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
奥社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = 里宮、奥社：[[国常立尊]] [[大己貴命]] [[少彦名命]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[県社]] |創建 = [[702年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年） |本殿 = 御嶽神社・里宮 |別名 = 御嶽神社王滝口、王滝口御嶽神社 |札所等 = [[#神社社殿・社務所|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所]]を参照 |例祭 = [[#祭事|王滝御嶽神社の祭事]]を参照 |神事 = 王滝御嶽神社の祭事を参照 |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Nagano|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=48|lat_sec=53.3|lon_deg=137|lon_min=33|lon_sec=7.2|position=bottom|label=里宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=35|lat_min=53|lat_sec=19.4|lon_deg=137|lon_min=28|lon_sec=57.8|position=right|label=奥社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|35.847240|N|137.530747|E}}|fullscreen-option=0|zoom=10|float=center|width=190|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E}}|label1=里宮|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E}}|label2=奥社|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} '''王滝御嶽神社'''（おうたきおんたけじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]にある神社。'''王滝口御嶽神社'''（おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ）、'''御嶽神社王滝口'''（おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち）ともいう。 [[木曽御嶽山]]への[[山岳信仰]]に基づく現地の神社（[[木曽御嶽神社]]）の1つであり、南方からの登山道である王滝口（おうたきぐち）沿いにある神社である（東方の黒沢口沿いにも[[黒沢御嶽神社|別の神社]]がある）。山頂に'''奥社'''、 山麓に'''里宮'''がある。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は以下の3柱。 [[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと） [[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと） [[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと） 主祭神以外については、社殿の各宮の項目を参照。 == 歴史 == [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]2年（[[702年]]）、[[信濃]][[国司]]の'''高根道基'''が[[御嶽山 (長野県)|木曽御嶽山]]頂上奥社を創建したのを始まりとする。 [[宝亀]]5年（[[774年]]）、信濃国司の'''石川朝臣望足'''が、[[光仁天皇]]の勅命を奉じて[[御嶽山 (長野県)|木曽御嶽山]]へ登山し、悪疫退散を祈願した。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]3年（[[925年]]）、'''白川少将重頼'''が木曽御嶽山へ登山し奥社の神殿を再建した。 [[応保]]元年（[[1161年]]）、[[後白河天皇|後白河上皇]]の[[勅使]]が木曽御嶽山を登山し参拝した。 [[文明 (日本)|文明]]16年（[[1484年]]）、一合目里宮社を再建。 [[文亀]]3年（[[1503年]]）、一合目里宮社を再興したと記録にある。 [[寛文]]4年（[[1664年]]）、[[山村代官屋敷|木曽代官]]の[[山村良豊]]が里宮社殿を再建。 [[天明]]2年（[[1782年]]）、[[覚明 (行者)|覚明]]が木曽御嶽山黒沢口登山道を開いた。 [[寛政]]4年（[[1792年]]）、[[普寛行者]]が木曽御嶽山王滝口登山道をそれぞれ開き、[[講社]]を作り軽精進潔斎による木曽御嶽山登山を奨励したことにより、御嶽講（[[山岳信仰]]・[[講]]）が全国に広まり、現在も[[御嶽教]]の信者による参拝が続いている。 == 社殿 == [[File:Ontakejinja satomiya0001.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社里宮]] [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 里宮社殿.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の里宮にある社殿]] [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 頂上奥社.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の王滝頂上奥社社殿]] *'''里宮'''（さとみや）一合目にあり、「本社（ほんしゃ）」「若宮（わかみや）」「岩戸権現（いわとごんげん）」とも呼ばれる。祭神は[[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと）・[[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと）・[[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと）。 *'''十二大権現'''（じゅうにだいごんげん）四合目下にあり、祭神は[[木花開耶姫命]]（このはなさくやびめのみこと）。 *'''八海山神社'''（はっかいさんじんじゃ）五合目にあり、祭神は[[国狭槌尊]]（くにさつちのみこと）。 *'''三笠山神社'''（みかさやまじんじゃ）七合目、三笠山頂上にあり、祭神は[[豊斟渟尊]]（とよくむぬのみこと）。 *'''田ノ原大黒天'''（たのはらだいこくてん）七合目にあり祭神は[[大国主命]]（おおくにぬしのみこと）。 *'''頂上奥社'''（ちょうじょうおくしゃ） 木曽御嶽山王滝口頂上にあり、祭神は[[国常立尊]]（くにとこたちのみこと）・[[大己貴命]]（おおなむちのみこと）・[[少彦名命]]（すくなひこなのみこと）。 ==社務所== *別殿社務所・一合目 *里宮社務所・一合目 *里宮拝殿・一合目 *八海山社務所・五合目 *田の原社務所・七合目 *遙拝所社務所・七合目 *頂上社務所・[[木曽御嶽山]]王滝口頂上 == 祭事 == [[File:御嶽神社王滝口 例大祭.jpg|thumb|御嶽神社王滝口の7月に行われる例大祭]] *年中行事 **1月1日 歳旦祭 里宮 **1月5日新年御神楽始め祭 別殿 **1月16日 厄除交通安全祈願祭 別殿 **2月1日 古神札焚上げ祭 里宮 **春分の日 春祭 里宮 **5月1日 合併神社祭 里宮
招魂社祭 招魂社 **6月30日 大祓式 別殿 **7月1日 開山祭 田の原 **7月10日 頂上奥社開山祭 頂上奥社 **7月27～28日 御嶽神社例大祭 里宮・八幡堂 **9月初旬 頂上奥社閉山祭 頂上奥社 **9月10日 講祖（[[普寛行者]]・[[覚明行者]]）本社祭 **10月第3土曜、日曜日 霊神社合祀慰霊大祭 里宮 **11月23日 秋祭 里宮 **12月31日 除夜祭 里宮 *毎日の祭事 **毎月1日および15日 月並祭 里宮・別殿 == 御嶽山信仰、行者、講社 == [[御嶽山 (長野県)#歴史・信仰|御嶽山の歴史・信仰]]および[[御嶽教]]を参照。 ===関連する神社・宗教法人=== *[[木曽御嶽本教]] *[[黒沢御嶽神社]] == 文化財等 == *古記録・絵画等 **[[祭文|古祭文]][[祝詞]]類 **王御嶽山清女行法巻 [[文亀]]3年（1503年） [[室町時代]]の古祭文 **角倉与一奉納絵馬 [[寛文]]8年（1668年） **千羽鶴絵馬 [[慶応]]2年（1866年） **龍虎の図 寛文4年（1664年） **天狗の面 **山村代官絵馬 **風流踊りの図 [[享保]]18年（1733年） *[[普寛行者]]ゆかりの品 **愛用の茶器と椀 **開山登山の節の護身用小刀 **普寛行者道中日記 **普寛行者一行に宛てた書状 **開山登山の節の護身用天狗の爪 == 現地情報 == [[長野県]][[木曽郡]][[王滝村]]3315番地 別殿（滝旅館内） *交通アクセス **車利用の場合 [[東京]]方面からは、[[伊那インターチェンジ|伊那IC]]下車、里宮まで約55分 [[大阪]]・[[名古屋]]方面からは、[[中津川インターチェンジ|中津川IC]]下車、里宮まで1時間50分 **列車利用の場合 東京方面・大阪・名古屋方面いずれからも、[[中央本線]][[木曽福島]]下車、バス利用で里宮まで約50分 == 関連項目 == *[[御嶽山 (長野県)]] *[[王滝村]] *[[御嶽教]] *[[百草丸]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} *[http://www.ontakejinja.jp/ 王滝御嶽神社]（公式サイト） == 参考文献 == * 王滝村史 *『寺と神社 (信州の文化シリーズ)』　御嶽神社　p249　信州郷土史研究会　 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]]　1981年 * {{Cite book|和書 |editor=菅原壽清、中山郁、時枝務 |year=2009 |month=8 |title=木曽のおんたけさん―その歴史と信仰 |publisher=岩田書院 |isbn=978-4872945690 |ref=木曽のおんたけさん―その歴史と信仰}} == 脚注 == {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうたきおんたけしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:王滝村の建築物]] [[Category:王滝村の歴史]] [[Category:山岳信仰]] [[Category:御嶽信仰]] [[Category:修験道]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya main hall|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso District, Nagano|qq=}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki, Nagano|qq=}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|qq=}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusha|qq=}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso District, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki era|qq=}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō era|qq=}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunki era|qq=}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun era|qq=}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei era|qq=}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei era|qq=}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki era|qq=}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō era|qq=}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunki era|qq=}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun era|qq=}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei era|qq=}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei era|qq=}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

{{神社 |name = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|qq=}} |image = [[File:Ontake-jinja Otaki Saromiya Honden.jpg|270px]]
{{ill|Satomiya main hall|ja|里宮の本殿拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya main hall|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso District, Nagano|qq=}}{{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki, Nagano|qq=}}3315番地（{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|ja|王滝御嶽神社別殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Betsuden|qq=}}） |location = {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=}}：{{coordinates|35|48|53.3|N|137|33|7.2|E|display=inline,title}}
{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusha|qq=}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso District, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki era|qq=}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō era|qq=}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunki era|qq=}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun era|qq=}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei era|qq=}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei era|qq=}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]

{{ill|Okusha|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okusha|qq=}}：{{coordinates|35|53|19.4|N|137|28|57.8|E|display=inline}} |deity = {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} |神体 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = 旧{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = [[702]]（{{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}}2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|qq=}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Ōtaki, Nagano|ja|王滝村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Kiso District, Nagano|ja|木曽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso District, Nagano|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}} (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|Taihō era|ja|大宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō era|qq=}} 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=}}, the governor of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=}}. In {{ill|Hōki era|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki era|qq=}} 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of {{ill|Emperor Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōnin|qq=}} to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōhō era|ja|応保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhō era|qq=}} 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}} 16 (1484) and {{ill|Bunki era|ja|文亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunki era|qq=}} 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in {{ill|Kanbun era|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun era|qq=}} 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in {{ill|Tenmei era|ja|天明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei era|qq=}} 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=}}, in {{ill|Kansei era|ja|寛政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei era|qq=}} 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines {{ill|Kuninotokotachi|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotokotachi|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Mountain worship]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]]
