Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[702]]（[[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]]2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

[[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

[[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} |神体 = | Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[702]]（[[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]]2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

[[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

link invalid}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}}, {{ill|Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Kiso Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|qq=|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|qq=|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|qq=|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Kiso Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E7%8E%8B%E6%BB%9D%E5%BE%A1%E5%B6%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] [[en:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]]

Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226513}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki
