Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}}

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}}

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}}

{{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}}

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = [[702]]（[[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]]2nd year ） |本殿 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja|御嶽神社・里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |別名 = {{ill|Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route|ja|御嶽神社王滝口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine Shrine|ja|王滝口御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 王滝御嶽神社|1=Ōtaki Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226513}} |札所等 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja|王滝御嶽神社の神社社殿・社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Buildings and Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |festival = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Festivals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |神事 = {{ill|Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Rituals|ja|王滝御嶽神社の祭事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtaki Ontake Shrine Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Ōtaki Ontake Shrine''' (王滝御嶽神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Ōtaki, Nagano|Ōtaki, Nagano]], [[:en:Kiso District|Kiso District, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is also known as '''Ōtaki Route Ontake Shrine''' ('''王滝口御嶽神社.''' おうたきぐちおんたけじんじゃ) or '''Ontake Shrine Ōtaki Route''' ('''御嶽神社王滝口,''' おんたけじんじゃおうたきぐち). It is one of the shrines associated with the {{ill|Ontake-kyō|lt=Mount Ontake faith|ja|木曽御嶽山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is located along the Ōtaki route (王滝口), a southern ascent to the sacred mountain. Another shrine, {{ill|Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|ja|黒沢御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|1=Kurosawa Ontake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929587}}, exists along the eastern Kurosawa route. The shrine complex consists of an {{ill|Okumiya|ja|奥社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okumiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥宮}} at the summit and a {{ill|Satomiya|ja|里宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satomiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the mountain's base. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] (Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}} (Ōnamuchi no Mikoto) * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (Sukunahikona no Mikoto) Other deities enshrined at the site are listed in the sections on the individual shrine halls. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:Taiho|Taihō era]] 2 (702) when {{ill|Takane no Michimoto|ja|高根道基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane no Michimoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], established the summit shrine (奥社) on {{ill|Mount Ontake|ja|木曽御嶽山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Ontake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}. In [[:en:Hōki|Hōki era]] 5 (774), {{ill|Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja|石川朝臣望足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Asomi Mochiashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the governor of Shinano, climbed the mountain under the imperial order of [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Emperor Kōnin]] to pray for the cessation of plagues. Over the centuries, various figures have rebuilt the shrine, including: * {{ill|Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja|白川少将重頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa no Shōshō Shigeyori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Enchō era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enchō era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} 3 (925) * A pilgrimage and restoration ordered by {{ill|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|ja|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Shirakawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōhō|Ōhō era]] 1 (1161) * A rebuilding of the Satomiya shrine at the mountain base in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei era]] 16 (1484) and [[:en:Bunki|Bunki era]] 3 (1503) * {{ill|Yamamura Yoshitoyo|ja|山村良豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamura Yoshitoyo|1=Yamamura Takatoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469484}}, a magistrate of the Kiso region, rebuilt the shrine in [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun era]] 4 (1664) The site played a key role in the spread of the {{ill|Ontake Pilgrimage|ja|御嶽講|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} movement in the Edo period. {{ill|Kakumyō|ja|覚明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakumyō|1=Kakumyo Tayubo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630332}}, in [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei era]] 2 (1782), opened the Kurosawa ascent route, and {{ill|Fukan|ja|普寛行者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}}, in [[:en:Kansei|Kansei era]] 4 (1792), opened the Ōtaki route. These developments allowed more pilgrims to ascend the sacred mountain. == Shrine Halls == * '''Satomiya''' (里宮) - Located at the first station, also called '''Honsha''' (本社), '''Wakamiya''' (若宮), or '''Iwatogongen''' (岩戸権現). Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * '''Jūni Daigongen''' (十二大権現) - Located below the fourth station. Enshrines {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. * '''Hakkaisan Shrine''' (八海山神社) - Located at the fifth station. Enshrines {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. * '''Mikasa Shrine''' (三笠山神社) - Located at the seventh station on Mikasa Mountain. Enshrines {{ill|Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|lt=Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumununo no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ}}. * '''Tano-hara Daikokuten''' (田ノ原大黒天) - Located at the seventh station. Enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. * '''Summit Shrine (Okusha)''' (頂上奥社) - Located at the summit of Mount Ontake. Enshrines [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]], {{ill|Ōnamuchi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}, and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. == festivals == * '''New Year's Day''' - Saitan Festival (歳旦祭) * '''July 1''' - Opening of the Mountain Festival (開山祭) * '''July 27–28''' - Main Festival (例大祭) * '''September (Early)''' - Closing of the Summit Shrine (頂上奥社閉山祭) * '''November 23''' - Autumn Festival (秋祭) == See Also == * {{ill|Ontake Faith|ja|御嶽信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽山|1=Mount Ontake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1754806}} * [[:en:Ontake-kyō|Ontake-kyō]] * {{ill|Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja|木曽御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiso Ontake Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御嶽神社 (木曽御嶽山)|1=Mitake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119929588}} {{神道 横}} {{translated page|ja|王滝御嶽神社|version=101697330|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:Ōtaki Ontake Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ontake-jinja (Otaki, Nagano)}} [[ja:王滝御嶽神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17226513}} [[Category:Architecture of Otaki Village]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Ōtaki Village]] [[Category:Mountain faith]] [[Category:Ontake faith]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
